verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1 / TMP21) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family. It cycles between ER, ERGIC, and Golgi as a COPI/COPII cargo receptor, but it is also found at the plasma membrane. Surface proteomics and cell-surface biotinylation studies have detected TMED10 on the outer leaflet of mammalian cells. Its architecture — a large luminal/extracellular domain, single TM helix, and cytoplasmic tail — places the N-terminal domain on the extracellular face when at the PM. This stable TM-anchored topology qualifies it as surface accessible.
